creator: linus.sundqvist@loopia.se
text:
Hi there,

Since OpenLDAP 2.4 seems to be more or less deprecated/not supported by upstream (https://www.openldap.org/software/download/) I would like an ldap-version flag to be added to default-versions.mk so that users can prepare moving to a newer OpenLDAP version (2.5 which is LTS, or 2.6 which is Feature release afaik).

Saw a suggestion from about a year ago here https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/openldap-dependency-with-powerdns.82456/

Currently ports are hardcoded to use 2.4 in /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.ldap.mk which makes it harder to try and migrate to a newer OpenLDAP version.
